cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about how the author introduced the topic in the passage. The question options are A to D. First, I need to look at the beginning of the text. The author starts with a series of questions: "Do you start your work by making a list of all you have to complete...?" Then there are more questions like "Do you always take a list with you to the supermarket?" and "Do you write down all the steps...?" So the author is using questions to engage the reader and lead into the discussion about to-do lists. The next part of the passage lists reasons why making to-do lists is beneficial, like giving memory a kick, helping stay in the moment, relieving stress, etc. But the introduction specifically uses those questions to set up the topic. So the answer should be A) asking a series of questions. The other options don't fit. B is presenting facts, but the author starts with questions. C is stating a point of view, but
